The passage mentions that Chinese is popular among foreign kids, and people around the world want to learn it because China is becoming an important country. It also says that foreign countries want to understand China better for business reasons, and the Chinese government is helping by sending teachers and setting up Confucius Colleges. Looking at the options: A) Because Chinese is getting more important in their schools. Wait, the first sentence says English is getting more important in schools, but Chinese is popular among foreign kids. So A might be a distractor. The passage says English is more important in schools, not Chinese. So A is probably not the right answer. B) Because Chinese characters have too many strokes. The passage actually uses this as an example of why it's difficult. Piao Chenglong says Chinese characters have too many strokes and that it's harder. But the question is why they want to learn it. So B is a reason it's hard, not why they want to learn. So B is incorrect. C) Because the number of people learning Chinese will be 800 million. The passage mentions that the number is expected to increase to 800 million, but that's a projection, not the reason people want to learn. It's more of a result rather than a cause.
